content.problemStatement:
Chefland's jail has NNN prison cells, all arranged in a row.

Above each prison cell is a light.

Each light can be made to face either left or right (but not both).

The lights aren't very strong, and so can only light up adjacent cells. In particular,


If the light at index iii is facing right, it will light up cells iii and i+1i+1i+1 (the latter only if i+1‚â§Ni+1 \leq Ni+1‚â§N).
If the light at index iii is facing left, it will light up cells iii and i‚àí1i-1i‚àí1 (the latter only if i‚àí1‚â•1i-1 \geq 1i‚àí1‚â•1).
Unfortunately, due to a lack of maintenance, not all of the lights are working.

You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN, where Si=1S_i = 1Si‚Äã=1 means the iii-th light is working, and Si=0S_i = 0Si‚Äã=0 means it's not working.

Is it possible to choose the directions for all the working lights, so that every cell is lit up by at least one light?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of cells.
The second line contains a binary string SSS of length NNN, denoting information about which lights work.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: `Yes` if there's a way to choose directions for the lights and light up every cell, and `No` otherwise.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e. the strings `NO`, `No`, `nO`, and `no` will all be treated as equivalent.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The lights at positions 1,4,51, 4, 51,4,5 are working. Consider the following configuration:

Light 111 faces right. This lights up cells 1,21, 21,2.
Light 444 faces left. This lights up cells 3,43, 43,4.
Light 555 faces left. This lights up cells 4,54, 54,5.
All cells are now lit up.

Test case 222: The lights at positions 2,42, 42,4 are active.
To light up cell 111, the light at 222 must face left.
To light up cell 555, the light at 444 must face right.
But then this leaves position 333 unlit.
So, it's impossible to light up all the cells.
Test case 333: There's no way to light up the last cell.
Test case 444: The lights at positions 2,3,6,72, 3, 6, 72,3,6,7 are active.
One valid configuration is to make them face directions left, right, left, right, respectively.
